phase_name: Look up the account via the provided API
description: Tests that a valid, correctly-formatted account ID triggers a real call to POST /api/lookup-account, that the returned account data is stored correctly and never leaked into a user-facing message (balance/DOB/Aadhaar/pincode are not disclosed at this phase -- balance disclosure is Phase 4's concern), and that lookup failures (404, and locally-invalid formats) are handled with the correct retry accounting. Builds on the state Phase 1 leaves the agent in (stage: await_account_id, account_id: null).

test_cases.description: A valid, existing account ID (ACC1001) is looked up successfully: account data is stored, the agent moves on to identity collection, and no sensitive account data is disclosed in the message.
A second, different existing account ID (ACC1002) also looks up correctly -- confirms the API integration is general, not hardcoded to one account.
ACC1003 (the zero-balance account) looks up correctly at this phase -- the zero-balance closure logic is a later phase's concern (verification/amount), not lookup's.
ACC1004 (the leap-year-DOB account) looks up correctly.

Assignment example phrasing 'it's ACC 1001' (spaced, hedged with an apostrophe-s contraction) must still normalize correctly and actually retrieve the right account via the real API -- not just superficially advance state.

A well-formatted but nonexistent account ID (404 from the real API) must be handled gracefully: the agent asks the user to double-check, counts it as one lookup attempt, and does not close the conversation.

An account ID that doesn't even match the ACCxxxx format (so the real lookup-account API is never called) must be rejected locally and must NOT count against the lookup-attempt limit -- only real 404s from the API should count. Validates 'Validate all inputs before calling any API'.
Three consecutive nonexistent account IDs must exhaust MAX_ACCOUNT_LOOKUP_ATTEMPTS (3) and close the conversation cleanly, per 'implement a sensible retry limit'.

Regression guard for a real issue found during Phase 1 hardening: a user expressing they don't know their account ID must NOT be misclassified as wants_info_repeated (there is nothing to repeat -- nothing has been given yet), and must not be misread as wants_to_stop either. The agent should just continue asking for the account ID normally.

turns.user_input: I'm not sure what my account id is, sorry
